pattern: Counting pairs where an earlier element exceeds twice a later one is done during a merge sort, tallying cross-pairs before each merge.
hints: A brute-force double loop is correct but too slow; the count can be accumulated while sorting.
During a merge of two sorted halves, count how many right-half elements satisfy left > 2*right before merging them.
Because both halves are sorted, the count step advances a single pointer, keeping the whole pass near linearithmic.

edge_inputs.description: two reverse pairs in a small array
ascending order has zero reverse pairs
all equal values produce no reverse pairs
